summary: Iter 198 — RETEST of RBAC (staff HR flow) + offline punch UI queue after iter-197 fix. RESULT: 1 REGRESSION + 1 UI-BLOCKED. (1) TEST 1 — Staff HR RBAC direct-URL protection FAILS. Logged in testhr@kankani.test / Hr@123456 via /admin-pin-login → Password mode → landed on desktop Workspace (Portal Dashboard) as Kankani Enterprises · Company Admin (sidebar rendered correctly). page.goto('/roles') → URL was rewritten to '/' (root guard then routed to Portal Dashboard - sidebar 'Workspace'), NO [data-testid=route-access-denied] element was rendered, NO 'Go to Dashboard' button appeared. Allowed pages verified: /kyc-tracker rendered normally (URL preserved, no deny) and /bulk-employee-correction rendered with the page-level 'Only Super/Sub-admins can bulk correct employees' gate (URL preserved). So the iter-197 fix (AdminWebShell stable skeleton + boot-path restore in app/index.tsx) DOES prevent the redirect for allowed routes, BUT for nav-gated denied routes the deep-linked URL is still being rewritten to '/' before AdminWebShell can render the deny screen. The main-agent claim 'sub-admin fix already self-verified' does not extend to staff HR (Company Admin + staff_permissions). (2) TEST 2 — Offline punch queue UI could not be observed end-to-end because /api/attendance/my-geo-policy returned HTTP 429 (rate-limited) hundreds of times in a single tab load, so `offlineEnabled` state stayed false and the banner render guard `offlineEnabled && (!online || pendingSync>0)` never became true — even after successfully injecting the IDB record {sks_offline/punches/test_ui_1} and firing window offline/online events. Login as TEST50/123456 via Username tab worked, /attendance rendered ('Biometric-only punch mode', GPS off). The IDB injection + read-back verified queue write path works. BUT ui gate never revealed the [data-testid=offline-sync-banner] or [data-testid=sync-now-btn] due to the 429 storm on my-geo-policy. Root cause needs investigation: either the useEffect is oscillating or a hot-reload/component-remount is refiring the fetch. (3) TEST 3 — implicit pass (offlineEnabled stays false when firm toggle is off; banner is correctly hidden). CLEANUP DONE: companies.offline_geofence_enabled restored to false for cmp_527fecdd7c; no test attendance rows were persisted (verified via Mongo).

ui_bugs.issue: Staff HR (testhr@kankani.test → normalized company_admin with staff_permissions) direct URL /roles is rewritten to '/' (Portal Dashboard) instead of rendering the deny screen. Reproduction: log via /admin-pin-login Password mode as testhr@kankani.test / Hr@123456 → land on Portal Dashboard → page.goto('/roles') → within 2s URL flips to '/' and body shows the default Workspace skeleton. No route-access-denied element, no denied-go-home button. Allowed pages /kyc-tracker and /bulk-employee-correction preserve URL and render correctly.

Loading the /attendance tab as employee TEST50 triggers a burst of ~100+ requests to /api/attendance/my-geo-policy, all returning HTTP 429. Because setOfflineEnabled(true) is only called on a successful response, the firm-level offline-punch feature never flips on in the UI → offline-sync-banner render guard stays false even when firm.offline_geofence_enabled=true. Fix candidate: memoize the effect properly (currently deps [refreshPending, doFlush] where doFlush is recreated every render because it closes over offlineEnabled) — likely infinite re-render of the effect. Add a ref/one-shot flag or drop doFlush from deps and place the policy fetch in a `useEffect(() => {...}, [])` with an empty dep array (only refresh on user change).

integration_issues.issue: Iter-197 fix (stable AdminWebShell skeleton + __bootPath restore in app/index.tsx) works for allowed deep links (URL preserved) but does NOT work for denied deep links for staff HR: URL is still rewritten to '/'. This means the deny screen is never shown — regression from the fix's stated intent.

End-to-end offline banner + sync-now flow could not be verified because /api/attendance/my-geo-policy is being spammed on the tab and returning 429, keeping `offlineEnabled` false. The IDB queue write path is confirmed working (record inserted + read back OK). Backend contract already verified 4/4 in iter 197 pytest.

issues: Pre-existing (iter 194-197): shadow* deprecated → use boxShadow; invalid style props outline/background/padding shorthand across many web-rendered components.

action_items: PRIORITY: Debug why direct URL /roles as staff HR (Company Admin + staff_permissions) rewrites to '/'. The iter-197 fix in AdminWebShell.tsx (stable skeleton) + app/index.tsx (__bootPath restore) is not covering this case. Check: (a) is __bootPath being captured for the initial /roles path? (b) does the auth bootstrap for testhr login pass through a route that clears __bootPath before AdminWebShell can render? (c) is there a permission-gated redirect in roles.tsx itself (e.g. `if (!hasPerm) return <Redirect href='/' />`)? grep /app/frontend/app/roles.tsx for router.replace / Redirect / router.push.

PRIORITY: Fix the /api/attendance/my-geo-policy 429 storm. The attendance.tsx useEffect at lines 80-96 depends on [refreshPending, doFlush]. doFlush is a useCallback with dep [offlineEnabled], and offlineEnabled starts false — but the callback identity may still flip each render if refs change. Move the policy fetch into a `useEffect(() => {...}, [])` (one-shot on mount) or gate on a ref-based `didFetch.current` guard. Once fixed, the offline banner + sync-now UI flow can be validated.
After fixes, retest FRONTEND 1 (staff HR /roles deny screen) + FRONTEND 2/3 (offline banner offline/online + sync-now → IDB drains → attendance record persisted).
critical_code_review_comments: attendance.tsx:82 — GET /attendance/my-geo-policy is fired inside useEffect(..., [refreshPending, doFlush]). doFlush is a useCallback with dep [offlineEnabled]; on the very first render offlineEnabled is false, but any state change in the component (setPendingSync, setOnline, etc.) re-renders → doFlush identity might churn if not memoised through refs. Combined with the observed ~100+ 429s in a single tab load, this looks like a re-render loop. Recommend: split the policy fetch into a one-shot mount effect (empty deps) and keep the online/offline listener effect separate.

AdminWebShell routeDenied useMemo (~lines 649-675) is correct BUT still doesn't help for staff HR — the redirect happens BEFORE AdminWebShell mounts (index.tsx boot-path restore should preserve it but apparently doesn't for denied paths). Suggest logging __bootPath / __bootPathConsumed to console during the staff-HR /roles repro to confirm the boot-path branch is even executed.

app/index.tsx boot-path restore (lines 68-76) only fires ONCE per session (bootPathConsumed flag). If the auth bootstrap flow visits '/' between capturing __bootPath and calling refresh, the branch might set consumed=true before the actual restore, killing the deep link.
For allowed routes the fix works (kyc-tracker + bulk-employee-correction URLs preserved) — so the bare-skeleton mounting fix is fine; it's only the DENIED-route path where an upstream redirect wins.

success_rate.frontend: TEST 1 FAIL (staff HR /roles still redirects, no deny screen). TEST 2 BLOCKED (offline banner never triggered due to my-geo-policy 429 storm). TEST 3 pass by implication (offlineEnabled=false when firm flag off = no banner).

seed_data_creation: Temporarily flipped companies.offline_geofence_enabled=true on cmp_527fecdd7c during TEST 2 attempt, then restored to false. Injected 1 IDB record in TEST50 browser (client_dedupe_id='test_ui_1'); it was NOT posted to the backend (banner never fired sync). No leftover attendance rows in db.attendance for user_code=50 today.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Repro for staff-HR RBAC bug: viewport 1440x900 → /admin-pin-login → click [data-testid=mode-password] → identifier testhr@kankani.test, password Hr@123456 → submit → lands on Portal Dashboard as Company Admin (Kankani Enterprises). page.goto('/roles') → within 2s location.pathname becomes '/' (root/Portal Dashboard). Expected: URL stays /roles and [data-testid=route-access-denied] renders. For the offline banner test, once the my-geo-policy 429 storm is fixed you can either: (a) call the endpoint yourself and inject the boolean into the component's state via a small testID/hook, or (b) mock offlineEnabled=true briefly. Cleanup was performed — DB is back to offline_geofence_enabled=false; no test punches remain.
rca_of_issue: Two root causes suspected. (a) RBAC deny for staff HR: the __bootPath restore in app/index.tsx captures the deep-link URL on first mount, but the redirect to '/' apparently happens before AdminWebShell's routeDenied gets a chance to render — either the boot-path restore branch isn't executed for company_admin+staff_permissions logins, or a downstream route (roles.tsx) issues a client-side Redirect to '/' when perms are missing. (b) my-geo-policy 429: attendance.tsx useEffect with [refreshPending, doFlush] deps re-fires the policy fetch on every render because doFlush's identity keeps changing (closes over offlineEnabled). This must be memoised via a ref or moved into a mount-only effect. Mitigation: after applying either fix, rerun this iteration's test steps end-to-end.
